sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jacqueline, presented to the office expressing anxiety about recent blood work showing hepatitis C.
She reported being surprised by the diagnosis as she has been feeling fine and was never previously informed of having hepatitis C.
She mentioned a history of IV drug use in the past, but has been clean for over 15 years.
She also reported a history of alcohol use, currently consuming about one beer a day, and smoking, down to one to two cigarettes a day.
She denied any other medical conditions.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression, which run in her family.
She has a family history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Review of systems: The patient reported feeling tired at the end of the day, despite getting enough sleep.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appeared in no distress and no jaundice on skin exam.
The heart exam revealed a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
The lung exam was clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
The abdominal exam showed present bowel sounds, a soft abdomen, and no hepatosplenomegaly.
Test Results: The patient's recent blood work showed a positive HCV antibody test, an elevated AST at 39, but normal ALT and total bilirubin levels.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has been diagnosed with hepatitis C, a viral infection that affects the liver, likely acquired years ago.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; Suspected liver disease; Suspected liver fibrosis.
Plan:
I ordered additional blood work to confirm the diagnosis with HCV RNA and HCV genotype testing.
I also ordered an ultrasound to determine the severity of liver disease.
I will use the results to determine the next steps for treatment.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in three weeks to review the results of the additional testing and proceed with treatment.
I instructed the patient to schedule appointments for her family members to undergo the same screening.
